response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web/full-stack and testing credentials, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. Large-scale infrastructure exposure exists (high-throughput low-latency services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, Kubernetes deployments), but it is DLang/Node-centric and totals well under 5 years. Formal technical leadership of a platform team is not evidenced, making the fit for a C++-heavy cloud compute platform lead role weak.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling authorship, and mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad technology range with cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) and long-running project ownership (GISCollective)
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 7 months professionally, versus the 8-year minimum requirement
No demonstrated technical-lead role owning platform strategy, cross-team alignment, or project priorities at scale
Infrastructure/compute-platform depth is limited; recent experience skews toward product full-stack and web development
